items.title: [正面] This is a super generous gacha with a fantastic ga...
items.summary:
This is a super generous gacha with a fantastic game behind it. 10/10.

Some players are upset with the developer for having creative control of the game. To those players my suggestion is: go write your own game, or play something else.

I really don't enjoy games that listen to the player base too much. You get that a lot in online competitive games where players insist one unit is underpowered or another overpowered, and demand changes for "balance" and thus begins the endless stream of nerfs and buffs that completely ruins the game.

Honestly, the core of this game is a ton of fun: a deck builder that allows you to decide how you want to try and beat each level. There's no "right way" to do things, and that to me is huge. Meta kills games.

Hats off to the developer for refusing to bow to the fans who think what they say is more important than what the dev wants.

items.title:
[正面] This game surprised me in a good way

You know the...
items.summary:
This game surprised me in a good way

You know the deal... you want something new to play but not pay for some premium AAA game which is highly likely to disappoint you so you troll the lists of free to play. Free to play gacha games are usually a red flag but hey it's free and they can't force you to pay for that 1 hour of fun you might have with it... 

just 1 hour...
turned into a couple of days now

A chtulu mythos gacha game that's heavy on the gacha but light on the monetisation.

In a unknown timeline Disollution has the potential to erase you from history to the point nobody even remembers that you existed and the Myrthag School for Awakers teach their students who can see this D-corruption to fight against a horrible and unspeakable fate.
If you like cosmic horror then there's just enough story to keep you hooked...juuuuuuuust look past some bad translation errors though.

If that doesn't bother you too much then this is basically a 4 character slay the spire in a darkest dungeons jacket. You make a team of 4 characters along a certain theme (Chaos, Caro, Aqeuer or Ultra; which basically come down to Wildcard, Vampire, Atlantis and Dimensional) with the theme seriously changing HOW you play the game... which is oddly refreshing.

As for the monetisation... yeah there's some but I've sunken plenty of hours in the game so far and haven't found the P2W aspect yet...nor spent a single dime and I AM getting the feeling everything is keeping up. EXCEPT my MC's level which decides your max hp (your team shares a lifebar) which does hold you back SOME but there's plenty to enjoy on the side IF you play this game ON THE SIDE; and if you do the energy mechanic won't bother you either as you will get plenty of energy potions to sate your "meh; just 1 more sidebattle" because it's only the sidestuff that requires energy.
As for the Gacha... 30 pull pitty system and you get PLENTY of pulls for free and can pay the ingame silver currency for even more (and get plenty of THAT currency too). The rest of the gacha is a wellknown trope by this point... Each pull gets you a chance for a character shard (or unlock if you pulled that char for the first time) and you get enCHANTS if you don't pull a char or enHANCE if you duplo-pull. Banner events have bigger chances for specific characters but the character lineup is small enough for a decent chance you'll pull what you want..especially considdering the pitty system.

Add to this all that the balancing of characters is done REALLY well and there's IS a meta but not in any way you will notice... maybe the Meta-attack character does 1 damage more per card than the #2 meta.

Play it as your sidegame and you'll have loads of fun. Hope the dev won't get greedy because this is definitely an underrated gem. Especially if they pay just a smidge more detail to the translations
